sources.name: Centre for Law and Policy Research — per-article URLs
sources.notes: Per-article pages parsed for the Version 2 (Constitution of India 1950) block; letter-suffixed slugs are excluded from the baseline as they are post-1950 insertions. CLPR labelling occasionally drifts (heading number ≠ slug number); the slug is treated as authoritative.

sources.name: Centre for Law and Policy Research — per-schedule URLs
sources.notes: 8 baseline schedules sourced; post-1950 9th/10th/11th/12th excluded. Schedule 7 List III was filled from the manuscript because CLPR carries only the post-1976 consolidated form.

sources.name: Lok Sabha calligraphic manuscript of the Constitution of India
sources.role: primary baseline fill (6 articles, 2 schedule sub-parts) + spot-check ground truth (5 articles)
sources.notes: The signed-as-adopted 26 January 1950 copy, calligraphed by Prem Behari Narain Raizada. Most authoritative 1950 text the project draws on. The PDF itself is not committed (gitignored due to size); the SHA-256 here lets contributors verify they hold the same artifact.

sources.name: Indian Kanoon — Constitution of India (offline save)
sources.role: cross-check + Phase 2 amendment metadata seed (95 distinct amendment-act citations harvested across 163 articles)
sources.notes: Akoma Ntoso–styled HTML. Carries the *current* consolidated Constitution with inline akn-remark editorial annotations citing amending acts. NOT a 1950 baseline source — useful for cross-checking unchanged-since-1950 articles, filling the unchanged subset of gaps, and seeding Phase 2.

sources.name: Bare Acts Live — Constitution of India
sources.notes: Current consolidated text with bracketed amendment annotations (`[X]` for inserted text, `[* * *]` for omitted text). Less complete than IK at the article level (361/395 bare-numeric articles present), but the bracketed annotations are useful for amendment-history work.

sources.name: Internet Archive — Aggarawala & Aiyar, 'The Constitution of India' (1950 commentary)
sources.role: scouting (initially mistaken for the 1950 official text)
sources.notes: A 1950 *commentary* by Om Prakash Aggarawala and S.K. Aiyar (Rajkamal Publications / Metropolitan Book Co., Delhi) — NOT the official Government of India publication. Discovered during Phase 1 scouting; the constitutional articles are quoted within the OCR'd commentary but the text is interleaved with extensive editorial notes and the OCR is noisy. Could still serve as a fallback for future gap fills.
